methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pivotal is a Growth-stage Transportation company founded by Marcus Leng. The Transportation sector peer universe comprises five identifiable private companies—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables available. This limited and heterogeneous peer set constrains traditional valuation methodology.

The Peer-Median approach is not applicable. A same-stage, same-sector cohort of three or more companies cannot be constructed from available data; filtering for stage alignment and comparable valuation tier yields fewer than the minimum three datapoints required for robust median benchmarking. The five Transportation peers identified vary substantially in business model, development stage, and capital structure, making sector-wide median multiples uninformative.

Given the absence of applicable triangulation methods, a supportable valuation range cannot be derived from the data provided. The analysis reveals a material constraint: Pivotal's valuation would require either (1) primary disclosure of at least two additional Growth-stage Transportation companies with transparent metrics, or (2) application of alternative methods (DCF, bottom-up market sizing, or stage-adjusted venture capital method) using Pivotal-specific operational data not present in this dataset. The Page backing and Growth stage suggest institutional conviction, but quantitative support remains pending additional comparable transaction or financial performance data.
